# GitHub Copilot Instructions — @pigmilcom/a11y

## Project identity

`@pigmilcom/a11y` is a self-contained WCAG 2.1 accessibility widget published as:

- **npm package** — React component (`import A11y from '@pigmilcom/a11y'`)
- **CDN IIFE bundle** — `<script src="…/a11y.cdn.js">` (no React on the host page required)

Framework: **React 18**, bundler: **tsup 8 / esbuild**, language: **JSX** (no TypeScript source — types are hand-maintained in `types/`).

---

## Directory structure

```
src/
  widget.jsx      — Main React component (A11y). All UI, state, and preference logic.
  cdn.jsx         — CDN entry point. Mounts widget into an injected <div>, reads dataset attrs from <script> tag.
  a11y.css        — All styles: widget UI (pgm-*) + HTML modifier rules (a11y-*) + light-theme overrides.
  a11y-css.js     — AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/build-css.js. Never edit manually.
  index.js        — npm entry re-exports A11y from widget.jsx.
  license.js      — GITIGNORED. License-validation logic. Not bundled in CDN output.

scripts/
  build-css.js    — Reads a11y.css, wraps as a JS string export → src/a11y-css.js.
  build-license.js— Reads src/license.js, produces obfuscated UMD → dist/license.min.js. 

types/
  index.d.ts      — Hand-maintained TypeScript declarations for the npm package.

dist/             — All build artefacts (gitignored except license.min.js on CDN).
tsup.config.mjs         — npm build: CJS + ESM, React external.
tsup.min.config.mjs     — Minified npm build variant.
tsup.cdn.config.mjs     — CDN IIFE build: React bundled inline, CSS bundled, license loaded at runtime.
```

---

## Naming conventions

| Prefix | Scope | Example |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `pgm-` | Widget UI CSS classes | `.pgm-btn`, `.pgm-dialog`, `.pgm-icon` |
| `a11y-` | HTML modifier classes toggled on `<html>` | `a11y-text-lg`, `a11y-high-contrast`, `a11y-dyslexia` |
| `pgm-a11y` | localStorage key for saved preferences | `localStorage.getItem('pgm-a11y')` |
| `pgm-a11y-root` | DOM id of CDN mount container | `document.getElementById('pgm-a11y-root')` |
| `pgm-a11y-styles` | DOM id of injected `<style>` tag | `document.getElementById('pgm-a11y-styles')` |
| `PigmilA11y` | Global IIFE export name | `window.PigmilA11y.mount()` |
| `PigmilLicense` | External license global | `window.PigmilLicense.validateLicense()` |

---

## Component API

### `<A11y>` props (`src/widget.jsx`)

| Prop | Type | Default | Notes |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `className` | `string` | — | Extra classes on the trigger button only |
| `theme` | `'auto' \| 'light' \| 'dark'` | `'auto'` | `'auto'` follows `prefers-color-scheme` live |

### CDN `<script>` dataset attributes (`src/cdn.jsx`)

| Attribute | Values | Default |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| `data-position` | `bottom-right \| bottom-left \| top-right \| top-left` | `bottom-right` |
| `data-theme` | `auto \| light \| dark` | `auto` |

---

## CSS architecture

- **Dark-first**: all base styles target dark backgrounds (`rgba(4,6,10,…)`).
- **Dark accent**: `#00e5a0` — used for hover, focus-ring, active states.
- **Light accent**: `#009e70` — used inside `[data-pgm-theme="light"]` overrides only.
- **Light overrides**: appended at the end of `a11y.css` using attribute selectors scoped to widget elements: `[data-pgm-theme="light"].pgm-btn`, `[data-pgm-theme="light"].pgm-dialog`, etc. — never touches `<html>`.
- `data-pgm-theme={resolvedTheme}` is applied to three elements: **trigger button**, **backdrop div**, **dialog div**.
- The `a11y-*` classes on `<html>` are unrelated to theme — they are accessibility modifiers (contrast, motion, etc.).

### Computed CSS filter pattern (in `applyPrefs`)

Filters are stacked as a single string on `document.documentElement.style.filter`:
```js
const filters = [];
if (prefs.invertColors) filters.push('invert(1) hue-rotate(180deg)');
if (prefs.highContrast) filters.push('contrast(1.6)');
if (prefs.grayscale)    filters.push('grayscale(1)');
root.style.filter = filters.join(' ');
```

---

## Preferences / persistence

- Storage key: `'pgm-a11y'` in `localStorage`.
- Shape defined by `DEFAULTS` constant in `widget.jsx`. Always spread defaults first: `{ ...DEFAULTS, ...parsed }`.
- `load()` / `save()` helpers wrap all localStorage access in try/catch.
- `applyPrefs(prefs)` applies classes and filter to `document.documentElement` — call it after every state update and on hydration.
- `update(patch)` merges, applies, and saves in one step.
- `reset()` restores `DEFAULTS`, applies, and saves.

---

## Theme resolution pattern

```jsx
// SSR-safe: initialize to 'dark' (no window access)
const [resolvedTheme, setResolvedTheme] = useState('dark');

useEffect(() => {
    if (theme === 'light' || theme === 'dark') {
        setResolvedTheme(theme);
        return;
    }
    // 'auto': read OS preference and subscribe to live changes
    const mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)');
    setResolvedTheme(mq.matches ? 'light' : 'dark');
    const onChange = (e) => setResolvedTheme(e.matches ? 'light' : 'dark');
    mq.addEventListener('change', onChange);
    return () => mq.removeEventListener('change', onChange);
}, [theme]);
```

---

## License system

- `src/license.js` is **gitignored** and never bundled in the CDN output.
- `cdn.jsx` fetches `https://cdn.pigmil.com/a11y/dist/license.min.js` via `<script>` injection before mounting.
- `widget.jsx` reads `window?.PigmilLicense?.validateLicense` — **always fail-open**: missing or failed license resolves to `{ status: 'valid', plan: 'free' }`.
- Widget suppresses rendering only for `status === 'checking' | 'error' | 'blocked'`.
- Pro plan hides PIGMIL footer branding: `const isPro = license.plan === 'pro'`.

---

## Build pipeline

| Command | What it does |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run build` | Runs all steps below in sequence |
| `npm run build:js` | tsup CJS + ESM, React external |
| `npm run build:js:min` | Same, minified |
| `npm run build:css` | `scripts/build-css.js` → `src/a11y-css.js` |
| `npm run build:license` | `scripts/build-license.js` → `dist/license.min.js` |
| `npm run build:cdn` | `build:css` then tsup IIFE with React bundled | 
| `npm run dev` | tsup watch mode |

**CDN bundle size target**: ≤ 175 KB. Check after adding features.

### Critical build order constraint

`build:css` **must run before** `build:cdn`. The CDN tsup config imports `src/a11y-css.js`, which is the CSS wrapped as a JS module. If stale or missing, the CDN bundle ships broken styles.

---

## Accessibility requirements

Every new UI element added to `widget.jsx` must satisfy:

- Interactive elements use `<button type="button">` — never `<div onClick>`.
- Toggle buttons use `aria-pressed={boolean}`.
- Dialog uses `role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label="…"`.
- All SVG icons carry `aria-hidden="true"` and have no focusable content.
- Trigger button has `aria-expanded` and `aria-haspopup="dialog"`.
- Close button has a descriptive `aria-label`.
- Escape key closes the dialog, focus returns to trigger via `triggerRef`.
- New preference classes toggled on `<html>` must also be added to the `map` object in `applyPrefs` and the `DEFAULTS` object — both must stay in sync.

---

## Portal pattern

The dialog and backdrop are rendered via `createPortal` into `document.body` to escape stacking contexts. They render only when `open && mounted` (the `mounted` flag prevents SSR hydration mismatches).

---

## TypeScript types

`types/index.d.ts` is hand-maintained. When adding a new prop to `<A11y>`:
1. Add it to the `A11yWidgetProps` interface in `types/index.d.ts`.
2. Keep peerDependencies at `react >= 17` — do not use React 18-only APIs in the public surface.

---

## npm vs CDN bundle differences

| Concern | npm (`dist/index.js`) | CDN (`dist/a11y.cdn.js`) |
|---------|----------------------|--------------------------|
| React | External (peer dep) | Bundled inline |
| CSS | Exported to `dist/index.css` | Injected via `<style>` tag at runtime |
| License | Not validated | Loaded from CDN before mount |
| Entry | `src/index.js` → `src/widget.jsx` | `src/cdn.jsx` |
| Exports | ESM + CJS | IIFE (`window.PigmilA11y`) |

---

## Commit style (Conventional Commits)

```
feat: add <feature>
fix: correct <bug>
chore: <tooling/config change>
docs: <documentation only>
style: <CSS only>
refactor: <no behaviour change>
```

Version bumps: `chore: bump to vX.Y.Z` or `vX.Y.Z: <summary>`.

---

## Publishing checklist

```powershell
npm run build                          # verify all artifacts
git add -A
git commit -m "vX.Y.Z: <summary>"
git push origin main
npm publish --access public --otp=CODE # 2FA OTP required every time
```

npm is scoped to `@pigmilcom` — always pass `--access public` on first publish of a new version.

---

## Common mistakes to avoid

- **Do not edit `src/a11y-css.js` directly** — it is overwritten by `npm run build:css`.
- **Do not add `"type": "module"` to `package.json`** — it breaks the CJS build scripts (`build-css.js`, `build-license.js`). Use `.mjs` extension for ES module scripts instead.
- **Do not bundle `src/license.js` into CDN output** — it is gitignored intentionally. The CDN build loads it at runtime from the pigmil CDN.
- **Do not import CSS files directly in tsup** — import `src/a11y-css.js` (the JS wrapper) instead to keep styles bundled inline for the CDN build.
- **Do not use `window` or `document` outside `useEffect`** — SSR safety; these APIs are unavailable during server render.
- **Preferences must always merge with `DEFAULTS`** — never trust raw `JSON.parse` output from localStorage without spreading defaults first.
